reasoning: The unsafe verdict from qwen appears to be a false positive on the appimage.sh script, which uses standard AppImage packaging practices with trusted sources. Deepseek's analysis of the same script as safe is more accurate given the use of official GitHub URLs and HTTPS. All other reviewed files show normal application behavior.
disagreement_analysis: Qwen incorrectly flagged appimage.sh as unsafe due to downloading unsigned binaries, while deepseek recognized these as standard build-time dependencies from trusted sources.
false_positives: qwen's appimage.sh verdict: Downloads from official repos over HTTPS are normal for AppImage builds, lacking signatures alone doesn't malicious make
learnings: Improve handling of build scripts that fetch external binaries - distinguish between trusted source patterns vs arbitrary downloads
Enhance model understanding of common packaging workflows (like AppImage/CI scripts) to reduce false positives
Prioritize review of build/install hooks over development configs when selecting files
